page: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8488C processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores operating at 3.2 GHz. The system includes 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The CPU architecture is x86_64 and incorporates a 105 MB L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

The underlying Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and INT8 operations. Cryptographic acceleration is supported via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI flags. Security features include Total Memory Encryption (TME), Indirect Branch Restricted Speculation (IBRS), and Speculative Store Bypass Disable (SSBD).

In benchmark evaluations, the c7i-flex.8xlarge demonstrates strong performance in several areas. It achieves top 25% results in Geekbench single-core and multi-core metrics, Passmark CPU score, and single-threaded gzip compression. Large-block memory write bandwidth and static web serving throughput also rank in the top 25%. Conversely, multi-core stress-ng CPU performance, cached read-only memory bandwidth, and database operations on Passmark fall within the middle 50% average.

Given its compute-heavy profile and 2.0 GB of RAM per core, this instance is suited for compute-bound tasks such as image processing, text processing, and small-scale language model inference, where it shows strong prompt processing and text generation speeds. However, the lack of local storage and moderate 6 Gbps network baseline may limit its utility for data-intensive storage workloads or high-throughput clustering. The flex-class designation offers a resource-dense option for workloads that do not require continuous maximum CPU utilization.
description: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.8xlarge is a compute-optimized x86_64 instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmark data shows strong performance in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, single-threaded compression, and static web serving throughput, while database operations and multi-threaded decompression perform in the average range. With a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps, this instance is suited for compute-intensive tasks, image processing, text processing, and small-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong single-core and multi-core benchmark performance.

tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and DDR5 memory for instruction-heavy workloads and text processing.

64.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 advanced instruction sets
Strong performance in single-core and multi-core Geekbench benchmarks
6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Suited for image processing, text processing, and small LLM inference
